description: Data-driven environmental analysis for a residential development — climate, comfort and daylight steering the massing from the first move.
body: A comprehensive environmental assessment for a ~6,500 m² site in a dense residential district of Palermo: two housing blocks and a public park shaped by climate analysis, evolutionary massing optimization (Galapagos), outdoor thermal comfort (UTCI), daylight factor, wind and sea-visibility studies — built on Ladybug, Honeybee and Infrared.city, with voxelization used for design-space exploration.

The optimized scheme reduces solar radiation on the façades by roughly 30%, reaches a 4% daylight factor against an initial 2.5% target, preserves sea visibility, and brings peak outdoor UTCI down from 37.3 °C to 35.5 °C through tree integration in the park.
